title: Normie mode (Groq) cannot access memory context — user feels Pi "doesn't remember" things
what_failed: In normie mode, Pi told Ash "I can't recall" about visa/university details that are stored in L3 memory. L3 context IS injected into the normie system prompt, but Groq's response didn't reflect it — either the context wasn't surfaced properly or Groq ignored it. User experience: Pi feels amnesiac even though data is stored.
where_failed: Normie mode session — Groq handoff, L3 context not being utilized
resolution: Three-part fix: (1) Updated prompts/consciousness_normie.txt — added explicit 'Memory access' section instructing normie to USE the ACTIVE CONTEXT block, changed 'recall past sessions' limitation to only gate deep search beyond ACTIVE CONTEXT. (2) Set use_split_prompt=True for normie ModeConfig so it routes through build_system_prompt_split() which includes mode block, timestamp, awareness inline, and L3 prefetch injection — same rich prompt as root. (3) Set prefetch_memory=True for normie ModeConfig so L3 memory is actually fetched and injected. Previously normie used _load_mode_prompt() (raw file read, no L3 injection). Now L3 ACTIVE CONTEXT appears in every normie turn.
